description: Psychological Intelligence Schemes for Expediting Surrender — the Allied intelligence operation housed at The White Visitation, tasked with psychological warfare and the investigation of paranormal phenomena for military use.

Allied Clearing House, Technical Units, Northern Germany — the intelligence unit nominally employing Slothrop during his time in the Zone, though its actual function remains characteristically unclear.
Allied Rocket Fuel — one of several acronymic organizations involved in rocket intelligence gathering in the Zone.
A former mental hospital on the English coast repurposed as headquarters for PISCES and various occult/psychological warfare programs. Home to Pointsman's Pavlovian experiments and séances alike.
British Special Operations Executive (SOE) — the intelligence service employing Pirate Prentice. Represents the old-boy network of British imperial intelligence.
The mysterious 'black device' — a special assembly installed in Rocket 00000, made of Imipolex G. The novel's central MacGuffin, sought by virtually every faction in the Zone.
The final V-2 rocket, serial number 00000, containing the Schwarzgerät and ultimately Gottfried as its human payload. Fired by Blicero in the novel's climactic/apocalyptic moment.

A fictional arousal-responsive plastic developed by IG Farben. Used both in the Schwarzgerät and allegedly in Jamf's infant conditioning of Slothrop. The material link between corporate chemistry, sexuality, and the rocket.
Slothrop's map of London showing his sexual conquests, which Pointsman discovers correlates with the map of V-2 rocket strikes — the mystery that launches the plot.

From Calvinist theology: those passed over by God, not chosen for salvation. In GR, the vast majority of humanity — the forgotten, the colonized, the expendable — as opposed to the Elect who control systems of power.

The chosen — those who benefit from and perpetuate systems of control. In GR, the corporate-military-intelligence elite: IG Farben, the rocket cartel, the intelligence agencies that survive and profit regardless of which side wins.

The resistance movement that forms in Part 4, opposing 'Them' — the Elect and their systems of control. Led loosely by Roger Mexico and Pirate Prentice, though Pynchon questions whether any counterforce can avoid becoming what it opposes.

German for 'end of burning' — the moment when a rocket's fuel is exhausted and it begins its ballistic arc. In GR, a metaphor for the transition point between powered ascent and inevitable descent, between control and gravity.

The massive German chemical conglomerate that manufactured everything from synthetic rubber to Zyklon B. In GR, represents the corporate power that transcends national boundaries and profits from both sides of any war.

The real-life cartel (1924–1939) of lightbulb manufacturers who agreed to limit bulb lifespan to 1,000 hours. In GR, Byron the Bulb's immortality is a rebellion against this planned obsolescence — a parable of corporate control over life and death.
